title: Door lock control experiment (15 subjects, 4 classes, 31 EEG ch)
description: A P300-based brain-computer interface dataset for home appliance control, comprising EEG recordings from 15 healthy subjects performing a visual oddball task with door lock control application. The dataset includes 31-channel EEG data sampled at 500 Hz, with target and non-target event classifications across 50 training and 30 testing trials per subject, acquired using active electrodes with linked mastoid reference.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a 31-channel actiCHamp system (Brain Products) with active sensors at standard 10-20 montage positions, sampled at 500 Hz with linked mastoid reference. Subjects performed a visual P300 oddball task with 750 ms stimulus onset asynchrony, presented on an LCD display with visual feedback. Each session included 50 training and 30 testing blocks per subject.
